reasoning: As of early November 2025, the Federal Reserve has implemented two 25-bps rate cuts during the year (one in September, one in October). There is only one FOMC meeting remaining in December. For the total to reach exactly four cuts, the Fed would need to make two more 25-bps cuts (or a single 50-bps cut) before the end of the year. However, recent communications from Fed Chair Jerome Powell suggest a possible 'break' and that another interest rate cut might be out of reach this year. Furthermore, the median FOMC projections point to only one more 25-bps cut (totaling three for the year), and a slight majority of officials favored only one cut in October and one in December. Thus, a 50-bps move in December, or an inter-meeting emergency cut plus a December cut, contradicts all recent guidance, making the probability of hitting exactly four cuts extremely low.
batch_queries: Will 4 Fed rate cuts happen in 2025?
